entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company whose organizational design must maintain firewall between candidate advocacy and employer representation

entities.name: Psychological Safety
entities.context: State maximized when there is structural separation of concerns between candidate advocacy and employer representation

entities.name: Separation of Concerns
entities.context: Structural principle that removes conflict of interest and enables deeper dialogue with candidates

entities.name: In-Group vs Out-Group Dynamics
entities.context: Psychological principle explaining why candidates perform sanitized versions of themselves when perceiving intermediary as employer agent

entities.name: Agent-Principal Problem
entities.context: Economic principle grounding the need for role separation in candidate-employer intermediation

entities.name: Candidate Advocacy
entities.context: One role in the structural separation - coach as ally enabling genuine self-reflection

entities.name: Employer Representation
entities.context: Other role in structural separation - must be kept separate to avoid contaminating candidate data quality

entities.name: Safe Space
entities.context: Product of architecture rather than just rapport - enables genuine candidate self-reflection

relationships.context: Organizational design must maintain firewall between advocacy and representation roles
Psychological safety is maximized when separation of concerns is maintained structurally
Grounded in psychological principle - coach becomes ally (in-group) rather than judge (out-group)
Addresses the agent-principal problem by removing conflict of interest
One side of the structural separation
Other side of the structural separation that must be kept distinct
Safe space is a product of architecture (separation) not just rapport
